name: Карта Таро — Фрирен
moment: Frieren stands full figure, SINGLE FIGURE, one clear frontal view only (no second view, no model-sheet, no alternate angle anywhere on the print), the figure occupying 76% of frame height, centered and upright in a still, composed stance, her long white twin-tail hair falling past her waist rendered in flat luminous ivory-white layers with soft grey shading lines, her calm half-lidded ancient eyes looking directly at the viewer with a serene unreadable expression, wearing her canon dark travel robe with a pale trim collar, both hands loosely wrapped around a tall wooden staff topped with a small pale crystal orb held vertically in front of her centerline, the staff's base touching the ground near her feet. ANATOMY: exactly two arms and two legs, correct human anatomy, no extra or duplicated limbs, no second figure or floating alternate pose anywhere in the composition. The entire scene sits inside a tall rectangular tarot-arcana card frame filling the canvas: a thick ornate engraved border of interlocking vine-and-star filigree runs continuously around all four edges, small mystical five-pointed star glyphs and crescent-moon glyphs are placed one in each of the four corners outside her silhouette, a thin star-dusted deep-indigo sky fills the background behind her with faint constellation lines connecting scattered pale dots, a single large pale moon glows softly directly above her staff's crystal orb without overlapping her face.
art_style: карта Таро (большой аркан) в духе классических оккультных колод конца XIX века без тёмной готики: плоские насыщенные заливки, тонкая гравюрная штриховка для теней, декоративная рамка из переплетённых лоз и звёзд, спокойная мистика — звёзды и полумесяц вместо черепов и когтей, светлая ровная композиция
typography: Main phrase 'THE MAGE' is set inside a narrow horizontal engraved title plaque spanning the width of the card directly below the frame's top border ornament (the traditional arcana-name plaque position on a tarot card, not a floating overlay), plaque fill flat aged parchment-cream with a thin double-rule engraved border (outer rule 0.03x cap-height, inner rule 0.015x cap-height, gap 0.02x cap-height), the phrase set in an elegant engraved serif display face with small decorative flourishes on the terminals, fill solid deep indigo-black, cap-height 0.09x figure width, word gap 0.05x cap-height so both words read clearly separated, each word appears exactly once. Directly below the card's bottom border, a small roman-numeral arcana number plaque reads 'XXI' set in the same engraved serif style, fill pale gold, cap-height 0.035x figure width, appears exactly once. The vertical katakana column フリーレン (Frieren, her name) is placed in the lower-right margin beside her trailing robe hem, outside the figure's silhouette, rendered as a bold flat pale-gold engraved column matching the card's ornamental gold linework, glyph size 0.06x figure width, one clean unbroken column of exactly five glyphs, gap between glyphs 0.04x glyph height, appears exactly once. No other text anywhere on the print.
palette: deep indigo night-blue, ivory white, pale gold filigree, soft lavender-grey shading, parchment cream plaque, muted star-silver
chroma_note: Фрирен вся в холодных индиго/белых/золотых тонах без зелёного — зелёный хромакей безопасен, при сведении проверить кромку белых волос по краю силуэта отдельно.

name: Спичечная этикетка — Аня Форджер
moment: Anya Forger stands full figure, SINGLE FIGURE, one clear frontal view only (no second view, no model-sheet, no alternate angle anywhere on the print), the figure occupying 74% of frame height, standing in a playful slightly bouncy stance with both feet planted, her pale pink bob haircut with two small decorative horn-like hair ornaments rendered in flat simplified poster shapes, her wide sly grinning expression with sparkling mischievous eyes looking directly at the viewer, wearing her canon dark green school uniform with a red bow at the collar, both arms raised slightly at her sides in a small triumphant gesture, both hands in loose open fists. ANATOMY: exactly two arms and two legs, correct human anatomy, no extra or duplicated limbs, no second figure anywhere in the composition. The whole scene is printed as a vintage 1950s Japanese matchbox label: a flat solid chroma-key green fills the entire canvas edge to edge, corner to corner, behind her with generous even margin on all four sides — nothing touching the frame edge, and the label's own decorative elements sit entirely INSIDE that green field as a smaller inset card (not touching the canvas border): a thin ruled aged mustard-cream paper card with rounded corners floats centered within the green field, sized clearly smaller than the full canvas so a visible band of flat chroma-key green frames the inset card on all four sides, the cream card carries a fine visible paper-grain texture and a few tiny scattered ink-speckle imperfections behind her, the whole print uses only three flat spot-colors plus the paper tone in true vintage label fashion, a small circular price-mark cartouche with a single stylized kanji numeral sits tucked in the lower-left corner of the inset card outside her silhouette.
art_style: винтажная японская спичечная этикетка 1950-х как небольшая карточка-вставка на сплошном хромакей-фоне: лаконичная плоская графика в 2-3 плашечных цвета плюс тон бумаги внутри вставки, зернистая текстура старой печати, простая рамка-паспарту вставки не касается края кадра, никаких градиентов и мягких теней — только чистые плоские формы и тонкий чёрный контур
typography: Main phrase 'WAKU WAKU' is set inside a small horizontal ruled banner strip at the bottom edge of the cream inset card directly above the card's own border rule, banner fill flat spot-color crimson with a thin single-rule cream border (0.025x cap-height), the phrase set in a rounded bold vintage poster-display face with slightly uneven hand-cut letter edges typical of mid-century label printing, fill solid cream-white, cap-height 0.08x figure width, word gap 0.05x cap-height so both words read clearly separated, each word appears exactly once. The small circular price-mark cartouche in the lower-left corner of the inset card contains a single stylized kanji numeral rendered in flat spot-color crimson-red (matching the banner, not green), cartouche diameter 0.09x figure width. The vertical katakana column アーニャ (Anya, her name) is placed in the upper-right margin of the inset card beside her raised hand, outside the figure's silhouette, rendered as a bold flat crimson-red column matching the price-mark color, glyph size 0.065x figure width, one clean unbroken column of exactly four glyphs, gap between glyphs 0.04x glyph height, appears exactly once. No other text anywhere on the print.
palette: aged mustard-cream paper, crimson red spot-color, sumi-ink black outline, soft pink hair tone
chroma_note: ИСПРАВЛЕНО после первого прогона: рамка-паспарту этикетки раньше шла ПРЯМО ПО КРАЮ КАДРА кремовым тоном — QC-гейт цвета рамки (dist=90.0 >= 70.0) справедливо забраковал попытку (рамка не хромакей), а вырезка по кремовому цвету-ключу съела куски лица/рук/платья персонажа. Теперь хромакей-зелёный явно идёт edge-to-edge по всему кадру, кремовая карточка-этикетка — вставка МЕНЬШЕ кадра, не касается края. Forest-green спот-цвет ценника/вертикали заменён на crimson-red (убирает риск слияния с зелёным хромакеем полностью, а не только 'при необходимости').

moment: Violet Evergarden stands full figure, SINGLE FIGURE, one clear frontal three-quarter view only (no second view, no model-sheet, no alternate angle anywhere on the print), the figure occupying 75% of frame height, standing in a graceful still poise with her weight gently shifted to one side, her long golden hair falling in soft loose waves rendered in transparent layered watercolor washes without hard outlines, her calm thoughtful gaze looking slightly downward, wearing her canon deep-blue Auto Memoir Doll uniform dress with a high collar and a small brooch at her throat rendered in a muted teal-blue tone rather than bright emerald, both of her articulated mechanical hands (rendered in soft pale ivory-metal watercolor tones, fingers delicately jointed) held gently in front of her, one hand resting lightly atop the other near a single ink pen she holds. ANATOMY: exactly two arms and two legs, correct human anatomy including her two mechanical hands, no extra or duplicated limbs, no second figure or floating alternate pose anywhere in the composition. The whole scene is rendered in soft transparent watercolor washes throughout: gentle bleeding color edges with no hard cel-shading outlines anywhere, a handful of loose paper-letter sheets flutter gently around her in the lower half of the frame as if caught in a soft breeze, fine watercolor ink-spatter droplets are scattered lightly through the background, a single white quill feather drifts near her mechanical hand, warm diffused backlight glows softly behind her whole silhouette without ever hardening into a graphic shape.
art_style: акварель и письмо: прозрачные акварельные заливки без жёстких контуров, мягкое перетекание цвета по краям, лёгкие акварельные брызги и разводы, летящие листы писем и перо как элементы композиции, мягкий рассеянный свет, никакой плашечной графики — всё живописно и воздушно

text_content.main: I want to know what love means

typography: Main phrase 'I want to know what love means' is set as flowing handwritten cursive script across one of the drifting letter-sheets in the lower third of the canvas, the letter-sheet rendered as a pale cream watercolor paper rectangle with soft torn-edge texture, the phrase in a delicate elegant handwriting-style cursive face with fine ink-line strokes, fill deep indigo-ink blue, cap-height 0.05x figure width (small and letter-like, matching genuine handwriting scale), word gap kept naturally tight as true cursive while remaining fully legible, the phrase appears exactly once as a single unbroken handwritten line. The vertical katakana column ヴァイオレット (Violet, her name) is placed in the upper-right margin beside her hair, outside the figure's silhouette, rendered as a soft watercolor-wash column in muted gold-ochre with gently bled edges matching the painting technique, glyph size 0.065x figure width, one clean unbroken column of exactly six glyphs, gap between glyphs 0.04x glyph height, appears exactly once. No other text anywhere on the print.
palette: golden hair wash, deep watercolor blue uniform, ivory mechanical hands, warm cream paper, soft ochre backlight, muted teal brooch accent
chroma_note: ВНИМАНИЕ: канонично брошь Виолетты изумрудно-зелёная — здесь она намеренно сдвинута в приглушённый тил-синий, но риск остаточного зелёного в отражениях акварели всё равно есть, поэтому хромакей для сведения фона на этом принте СИНИЙ (chroma: blue).

name: Обложка метал-альбома — Аста
moment: Asta stands full figure, SINGLE FIGURE, one clear frontal three-quarter view only (no second view, no model-sheet, no alternate angle anywhere on the print), the figure occupying 79% of frame height, planted in a wide aggressive battle stance, his short spiky ash-grey hair and the plain white headband tied across his forehead rendered in flat high-contrast poster layers, his wide fierce grinning expression showing gritted teeth, wearing his canon black-and-white sleeveless coat with tattered hem edges, both hands gripping the long hilt of his massive black anti-magic greatsword raised diagonally across his body with the blade's tip pointing up past his shoulder, the enormous plain matte-black blade rendered with a single sharp silver edge-highlight and no glowing runes or occult markings. ANATOMY: exactly two arms and two legs, correct human anatomy, the greatsword is a separate held weapon and does not replace or duplicate his limbs, no extra or duplicated limbs, no second figure anywhere in the composition. The whole scene is composed as an aggressive metal-album cover filling the canvas: jagged lightning-bolt graphic streaks radiate outward from behind his raised blade toward the corners of the frame, sharp thorned-spike ornament shapes frame the lower edge of the composition beneath his boots, the background is a flat stormy charcoal-black with faint distant lightning-flash silver streaks, a thin engraved silver border frames the entire album-cover canvas.
art_style: обложка метал-альбома: агрессивный графичный стиль без градиентов, зубчатые молнии и шипы-орнаменты как элементы обложки, чёрно-серебряная плашечная палитра с одним акцентным цветом, резкие плакатные контрасты света и тени, металлический леттеринг с шипами и молниями остаётся при этом читаемым
typography: Main phrase 'BLACK BULLS' is set as a large aggressive metal-band-style display lettering block spanning the width of the canvas across the lower third beneath Asta's boots, letterforms built from sharp angular strokes with small thorn-spike extensions and thin jagged lightning-crack details integrated into the stroke edges while keeping every letter fully legible (no illegible spike-overload), fill solid gunmetal-silver with a thin black bevel edge, cap-height 0.1x figure width, word gap 0.055x cap-height so both words read clearly separated, each word appears exactly once. The vertical katakana column アスタ (Asta, his name) is placed in the upper-right margin beside his raised blade, outside the figure's silhouette, rendered as a bold flat gunmetal-silver column matching the main lettering style with a thin lightning-crack accent line beside it, glyph size 0.075x figure width, one clean unbroken column of exactly three glyphs, gap between glyphs 0.04x glyph height, appears exactly once. No other text anywhere on the print.
palette: matte black, gunmetal silver, charcoal storm-grey background, white headband accent, single steel-blue lightning highlight
chroma_note: Палитра Асты (чёрный/серебро/пепельно-серый/белый) без зелёного и без насыщенных тёмных хоррор-элементов (меч описан как plain matte-black anti-magic greatsword, без свечения рун и слова demon) — зелёный хромакей безопасен, персонаж светлый и не рискует блокировкой safety-фильтра.
